title: Juvenile humpback whale washes ashore in Winthrop
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A juvenile humpback whale washed ashore in Winthrop, becoming lodged under a private dock along Washington Avenue.
WDC-NA and Massachusetts officials are investigating the whale's death, with plans for a necropsy pending.
This is the first large whale stranding in Massachusetts this year; six strandings were recorded in 2024.

title: Jahmai Jones hits memorable first home run for Red Sox
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jahmai Jones hit a two-run homer over the Green Monster in his second Red Sox plate appearance.
Jones, traded to the Red Sox, debuted his Fenway Park home run against the Tampa Bay Rays on July 18, 2026.
Previously struggling with a .135 average in 2026, Jones aims for a fresh start contributing as a utility player.

title: Wilyer Abreu joins elite Red Sox company following his back-to-back multi-HR games
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Wilyer Abreu became the first Red Sox player since Mookie Betts in 2016 to hit back-to-back multi-home run games.
Abreu is only the second Red Sox to achieve consecutive multi-HR games at Fenway Park, after George Scott in 1971.
Abreu's performance helped lift the Red Sox over the Tampa Bay Rays, moving Boston over .500 in the standings.

title: Red Sox extend season-altering win streak to 12 games in epic comeback fashion
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox extended their win streak to 12 games with a comeback victory against the Tampa Bay Rays.
Wilyer Abreu hit his fourth home run in two games, keying a late rally from a 6-3 deficit.
Boston holds a wild-card spot in the American League, with one game ahead of the Minnesota Twins.

title: Report: LeBron James ‘hates’ Celtics, would rank them last on his list of teams to join
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James reportedly ranks the Boston Celtics last on his list for potential free agency destinations.
ESPN's Brian Windhorst reports James hates Boston, citing long-standing rivalries and negative fan experiences.
James's preferred teams include the Heat, Cavaliers, and Warriors as he weighs a final NBA season move.

title: Tom Brady busy at Fanatics Fest, from a face slap to showing off his arm
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tom Brady slapped Logan Paul at Fanatics Fest 2026 in New York City, leading to a viral moment.
The slap followed a months-long playful feud over athleticism and flag football championship results.
Aside from the slap, Brady showcased his athletic skills, playing baseball and golf at the event.

title: 4 charged in connection with alleged kidnapping, beating near Nantucket park
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Four men from Nantucket were charged with kidnapping and assault after allegedly attacking a man near Winter Park.
The 28-year-old victim was beaten, tied with an extension cord, and forced into a residence by the suspects.
The accused, identified as Brown, Hazel, Lawrence, and Barnett, are out on bail and facing multiple charges.

title: 2 arrested at Logan Airport as ICE activity ramps up
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Maria Rosales and David Ardila were arrested at Logan Airport by ICE during target operations.
Rosales is a DACA recipient from Colombia, and Ardila is a Venezuelan asylum seeker who overstayed his visa.
Attorney Todd Pomerleau reports about 10 immigration-related arrests at Logan Airport in the past three months.

title: 5 movies to watch after ‘The Odyssey’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: "Interstellar" on Hulu and Paramount+ echoes themes seen in "The Odyssey," focusing on time-bending space travel.
"O Brother, Where Art Thou?" offers a humorous adaptation of Homer’s epic, available on Criterion Channel.
"The SpongeBob SquarePants Movie" draws inspiration from "The Odyssey" with its aquatic hero's journey.

title: Formidable Red Sox extend win streak to 11 games with doubleheader sweep of Rays
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox extended their winning streak to 11 games after sweeping the Tampa Bay Rays in a doubleheader.
In Game 1, the Red Sox won 10-0 with contributions from Jarren Duran, Carlos Narvaez, and pitcher Jake Bennett.
Game 2 saw Boston win 5-3, highlighted by home runs from Wilyer Abreu and Willson Contreras, improving their record to 48-48.
